text: Which of the following activities consume virtual warehouse credits in the Snowflake environment? (Choose two.)
When loading data into Snowflake, the COPY command supports which of the following?
What is cached during a query on a virtual warehouse?

options.b: Any columns accessed during the query
options.c: The columns in the result set of the query
options.d: All rows accessed during the query

text: What is the default character set used when loading CSV files into Snowflake?
Which of the following describes external functions in Snowflake?

options.a: They are a type of User-defined Function (UDF).
options.b: They contain their own SQL code.
options.c: They call code that is stored inside of Snowflake.
options.d: They can return multiple rows for each row received.

text: Which of the following are valid methods for authenticating users for access into Snowflake? (Choose three.)
Which condition will trigger a cluster to shut-down in a standard multi-cluster warehouse auto-scaling policy?

options.a: When after 2-3 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the most-loaded cluster could be redistributed.
options.b: When after 5-6 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the most-loaded cluster could be redistributed.
options.c: When after 5-6 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the least-loaded cluster could be redistributed.
options.d: When after 2-3 consecutive checks the system determines that the load on the least-loaded cluster could be redistributed.

text: What is the minimum Snowflake edition needed for database failover and fail-back between Snowflake accounts for business continuity?
How would a user execute a series of SQL statements using a task?

options.a: Include the SQL statements in the body of the task separated by semicolons.
options.b: A task can only have one DML statement per invocation.
options.c: Use a stored procedure executing multiple SQL statements and invoke the stored procedure from the task.
options.d: Create a task for each SQL statement and string them together sequentially.

text: How many resource monitors can be assigned at the account level?
Data storage for individual tables can be monitored using which commands and/or objects? (Choose two.)
How would a user run a multi-cluster warehouse in maximized mode?

options.a: Configure the maximum clusters setting to 'Maximum'.
options.b: Turn on the additional clusters manually after starting the warehouse.
options.c: Set the minimum clusters and maximum clusters settings to the same value.
options.d: Set the minimum clusters and maximum clusters settings to different values.

text: What internal stages are available in Snowflake? (Choose three.)
Which stages are used with the Snowflake PUT command to upload files from a local file system? (Choose three.)
Which data type can store more than one type of data structure?
User-level network policies can be created by which of the following roles? (Choose two.)
What SQL command would be used to view all roles that were granted to USER1?
Which ACCOUNT_USAGE views are used to evaluate the details of dynamic data masking? (Choose two.)
Which of the following are considerations when using a directory table when working with unstructured data?

options.a: Directory tables can only be used with internal stages.
options.b: Directory tables allow for cataloging and querying unstructured data files in cloud storage.
options.c: A warehouse is required to query the directory table.
options.d: Directory tables are a separate object and not part of a stage definition.

text: True or False: Snowflake supports the ingestion of data from on-premises stages directly using Snowpipe.
Which of the following statements is true regarding micro-partitions?

options.a: They allow for extremely large tables to be scanned efficiently through pruning.
options.b: Users must manually define the partition boundaries.
options.c: They are typically between 500 MB and 2 GB in size.
options.d: They only exist in the Business Critical edition.

text: What is the primary function of the Object Tagging feature?

options.a: To provide a way to group and track objects for governance and cost management.
options.b: To speed up query performance by tagging frequently used tables.
options.c: To encrypt sensitive data at the column level.
options.d: To share data with external partners.

text: True or False: A Snowflake account can be moved between cloud providers after it has been created.
Which Snowflake object is used to automate the execution of a Stored Procedure on a schedule?
What is the impact of zero-copy cloning on storage costs?

options.b: No additional storage cost is incurred until data in the clone or original is modified.
options.c: Cloning is a free feature and never incurs storage costs.
options.d: Storage costs are reduced because of compression.

text: Which function is used to convert a VARIANT column into a relational table structure?
True or False: Snowflake provides the ability to undrop a schema even if the database it belongs to has not been undropped.
Which view provides the query-level details of credit consumption for serverless features like Auto-Clustering?
What is the result of a 'Scale Up' operation on a Virtual Warehouse?

options.a: Adding more clusters to handle concurrency.
options.b: Increasing the T-shirt size to handle more complex or larger queries.
options.c: Moving data to a faster storage tier.
options.d: Automatically suspending the warehouse when not in use.

text: Which of the following is a requirement for a 'Secure Share'?

options.a: The data provider and consumer must be in the same region.
options.b: The consumer must have a Business Critical edition account.
options.c: The provider must use the ACCOUNTADMIN role to create the share.
options.d: The provider and consumer must be on the same cloud platform.

text: True or False: Time Travel can be configured to 0 days, effectively disabling it.
Which of the following are supported semi-structured data formats? (Choose three.)
What does 'Fail-safe' storage protect against?

options.a: Accidental deletion of data by a user.
options.b: Hardware failures or data corruption within the Snowflake platform.
options.c: System downtime during a cloud provider outage.

text: Which command allows you to view the stages available in your current database/schema?
True or False: Snowflake allows the creation of a 'Task' that has no schedule but is triggered by another task.
What is the benefit of the 'Snowflake Marketplace'?

options.a: It allows users to buy and sell Snowflake credits.
options.b: It provides a platform to discover and access third-party datasets and services without moving data.
options.c: It is a portal for downloading Snowflake drivers.
options.d: It is where users can buy support tickets.

text: Which role is the only one that can see the 'Billing' tab in the Snowflake UI by default?
True or False: Data in a 'Temporary' table is encrypted.
What is the function of 'Clustering Keys'?

options.a: To ensure that data is encrypted using multiple keys.
options.b: To group similar data rows into the same micro-partitions to improve pruning.
options.c: To connect multiple Snowflake accounts into a cluster.
options.d: To manage the metadata of a database.

text: Which of the following can be used as a 'Stream' source? (Choose two.)
True or False: The 'PUT' command can be used to upload files to an External Stage.
What is the purpose of 'PrivateLink' with Snowflake?

options.a: To provide a faster internet connection to the Snowflake UI.
options.b: To ensure that all traffic between the customer's network and Snowflake stays on the cloud provider's internal backbone.
options.c: To link two different Snowflake accounts privately.
options.d: To encrypt data using a private key.

text: Which parameter defines how long a Virtual Warehouse stays active after the last query finishes before suspending?
True or False: You can use 'Snowpark' to write data processing logic in Python, Java, or Scala.
Which view displays the list of all current 'Resource Monitors'?
What happens if you run a 'CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE' command on a table with existing data?

options.a: The data is preserved and the schema is updated.
options.b: The table is dropped and a new empty table is created.
options.c: An error is returned because the table is not empty.
options.d: The table is renamed and a new one is created.

text: Which Snowflake edition is the first to offer 'Column-Level Security' features like Masking Policies?
True or False: Snowflake handles the rotation of encryption keys automatically.
Which command allows you to view the Query Profile for a query that ran in the past?

options.c: No command; it must be viewed in the Snowflake Web UI.

text: What is the maximum file size supported by the 'Snowflake Web UI' for data loading?
